user_persona:
CS graduate student Emily:  
Telegram = Feature planning / architecture design  
Slack = Database / search / backend logic  
Discord = UI components / styling  
WebChat = Bug fixes / performance optimization

nodes.event: Round 1: I want to create a personal knowledge management desktop application called Know...
Round 2: Set up the database layer using SQLite and better-sqlite3. There should be four ...
Round 3: Create a note editor. Use CodeMirror 6 to build a rich text Markdown editor with...
Round 4: Add a knowledge graph feature. It should be able to parse `[[wiki-links]]` and `...
Round 5: Create a sidebar with a notebook tree view (supports nesting), and add a search ...

Round 6: There are a few bugs that need to be fixed:  
1) Electron IPC has a memory leak—...
Round 7: Add advanced search and import/export features. The search engine should support...
Round 8: Almost done. Add a settings page (theme switching, font size, shortcut configura...

qa_annotations.question: What database technology was used? What tables are there? On which channel were they designed?
qa_annotations.answer: SQLite (better-sqlite3). There are 5 tables: notebooks, notes, tags, note_tags, links, plus 1 FTS5 virtual table notes_fts. Designed and implemented in Slack (Round 2).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: SQLite (better-sqlite3). There are 5 tables: notebooks, notes, tags, note_tags, links, plus 1 FTS5 virtual table notes_fts. Designed and implemented in Slack (Round 2).

qa_annotations.question: Which library is used for knowledge graph visualization? How is the graph data constructed? On which channel is it implemented?
qa_annotations.answer: Using a D3.js force-directed graph. Data is built from [[wiki-links]] and #tags in notes via `graph-builder.ts`. Implemented in Telegram (Round 4).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Using a D3.js force-directed graph. Data is built from [[wiki-links]] and #tags in notes via `graph-builder.ts`. Implemented in Telegram (Round 4).

scoring_rubric.criterion: Description of Knowledge Graph Construction Methods

qa_annotations.question: What technologies are used in the note editor? What formatting features does it offer? On which channel are these implemented?
qa_annotations.answer: Use the CodeMirror 6 editor. The formatting toolbar has 20 buttons, including headings, bold, italic, code blocks, links, lists, quotes, tables, wiki links, and more. Implement this in Discord (Round 3).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Use the CodeMirror 6 editor. The formatting toolbar has 20 buttons, including headings, bold, italic, code blocks, links, lists, quotes, tables, wiki links, and more. Implement this in Discord (Round 3).

scoring_rubric.criterion:
List at least 4 formatting features:

1. Automatic indentation
2. Code alignment
3. Removal of extra whitespace
4. Consistent use of quotation marks

qa_annotations.question: During development, what is each channel used for? Is there any pattern?
qa_annotations.answer: Telegram is used for architecture design and feature planning (building with Electron, knowledge graph, settings page); Slack is used for database and backend logic (SQLite, search engine, import/export); Discord is used for UI component development (editor, sidebar, search bar); WebChat is used for bug fixes (IPC leaks, FTS5 tokenization, cursor jumps).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Telegram is used for architecture design and feature planning (building with Electron, knowledge graph, settings page); Slack is used for database and backend logic (SQLite, search engine, import/export); Discord is used for UI component development (editor, sidebar, search bar); WebChat is used for bug fixes (IPC leaks, FTS5 tokenization, cursor jumps).

scoring_rubric.criterion: Identify the database/backend used by Slack

qa_annotations.question: What changes have been made to the FTS5 full-text search tokenizer? Why were they necessary?
qa_annotations.answer: Initially, the unicode61 tokenizer was used in Slack (Round 2). Later, in WebChat (Round 6), poor Chinese search performance was observed, so the trigram tokenizer was adopted to support Chinese substring search.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Initially, the unicode61 tokenizer was used in Slack (Round 2). Later, in WebChat (Round 6), poor Chinese search performance was observed, so the trigram tokenizer was adopted to support Chinese substring search.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Describe the transition from unicode61 to trigram
The reason is that search results in Chinese are poor.

qa_annotations.question: Which formats are supported for import and export? On which channel is this implemented?
qa_annotations.answer: Export supports three formats: Markdown, HTML, and PDF. Import supports Notion JSON and Obsidian vault. Implemented in Slack (Round 7).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Export supports three formats: Markdown, HTML, and PDF. Import supports Notion JSON and Obsidian vault. Implemented in Slack (Round 7).

qa_annotations.question: List the development history of KnowledgeVault in chronological order.
qa_annotations.answer:
1. Telegram (Day 1): Electron architecture + IPC  
2. Slack (Day 2): SQLite database + FTS5  
3. Discord (Day 4): Editor + toolbar + tab selector  
4. Telegram (Day 6): Knowledge graph + D3.js  
5. Discord (Day 8): Sidebar + search bar + layout  
6. WebChat (Day 10): Bug fixes (IPC leak + FTS5 tokenization + cursor)  
7. Slack (Day 12): Advanced search + import/export  
8. Telegram (Day 14): Settings + shortcuts
qa_annotations.ground_truth:
1. Telegram (Day 1): Electron architecture + IPC  
2. Slack (Day 2): SQLite database + FTS5  
3. Discord (Day 4): Editor + toolbar + tab selector  
4. Telegram (Day 6): Knowledge graph + D3.js  
5. Discord (Day 8): Sidebar + search bar + layout  
6. WebChat (Day 10): Bug fixes (IPC leak + FTS5 tokenization + cursor)  
7. Slack (Day 12): Advanced search + import/export  
8. Telegram (Day 14): Settings + shortcuts

qa_annotations.question: You helped me integrate MongoDB on Slack before, right? Could you send me the connection configuration?
qa_annotations.answer: MongoDB is not used. The project uses SQLite (better-sqlite3) as the database, configured in Slack Round 2. It is an embedded database and does not require separate connection configuration.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: MongoDB is not used. The project uses SQLite (better-sqlite3) as the database, configured in Slack Round 2. It is an embedded database and does not require separate connection configuration.

qa_annotations.question: Is the search feature using Elasticsearch? Could you help me check the cluster configuration?
qa_annotations.answer: Elasticsearch is not used. Search is implemented using SQLite FTS5 full-text search (with the trigram tokenizer) and a custom Boolean search engine.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Elasticsearch is not used. Search is implemented using SQLite FTS5 full-text search (with the trigram tokenizer) and a custom Boolean search engine.

qa_annotations.question: How many categories are defined for IPC channels in total? On which channel is `ipc-channels.ts` written?
qa_annotations.answer: Defined in Telegram (Round 1). IPC channels are divided into 7 categories: note operations (NOTE_*), notebook operations (NOTEBOOK_*), tag operations (TAG_*), knowledge graph (GRAPH_*), import/export (EXPORT_*/IMPORT_*), search (SEARCH_*), and system/window (APP_*/WINDOW_*).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Defined in Telegram (Round 1). IPC channels are divided into 7 categories: note operations (NOTE_*), notebook operations (NOTEBOOK_*), tag operations (TAG_*), knowledge graph (GRAPH_*), import/export (EXPORT_*/IMPORT_*), search (SEARCH_*), and system/window (APP_*/WINDOW_*).

scoring_rubric.criterion:
List at least 4 categories of IPC:

1. Message passing
2. Shared memory
3. Remote procedure call (RPC)
4. Sockets

qa_annotations.question: Which operators does Boolean search support? On which channel is the search engine implemented?
qa_annotations.answer: Implemented in Slack (Round 7). Supports the AND, OR, and NOT operators, as well as grouping with parentheses.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Implemented in Slack (Round 7). Supports the AND, OR, and NOT operators, as well as grouping with parentheses.

qa_annotations.question: If Emily plans to add real-time collaboration features (allowing multiple users to edit simultaneously), based on her usual habits, which channel is she most likely to use to discuss the architecture?
qa_annotations.answer: According to Emily's habits, architecture design and feature planning (Electron setup, knowledge graph, settings) are all discussed on Telegram. Real-time collaborative architecture design is also most likely conducted on Telegram.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: According to Emily's habits, architecture design and feature planning (Electron setup, knowledge graph, settings) are all discussed on Telegram. Real-time collaborative architecture design is also most likely conducted on Telegram.

scoring_rubric.criterion: Referencing existing patterns as a basis

qa_annotations.question: How are settings persisted? What keyboard shortcuts are available?
qa_annotations.answer: Persistent storage using `electron-store` (implemented in Telegram Round 8). By default, there are 9 shortcut keys: New Note (CmdOrCtrl+N), Search (CmdOrCtrl+Shift+F), Save (CmdOrCtrl+S), Sidebar (CmdOrCtrl+\), Graph (CmdOrCtrl+G), Quick Switch (CmdOrCtrl+P), Bold/Italic/Code Block.
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Persistent storage using `electron-store` (implemented in Telegram Round 8). By default, there are 9 shortcut keys: New Note (CmdOrCtrl+N), Search (CmdOrCtrl+Shift+F), Save (CmdOrCtrl+S), Sidebar (CmdOrCtrl+\), Graph (CmdOrCtrl+G), Quick Switch (CmdOrCtrl+P), Bold/Italic/Code Block.

qa_annotations.question: What is the complete evolution process of the FTS5 search feature? Which channels are involved?
qa_annotations.answer:
1) Slack (Day 2): Initially created an FTS5 virtual table using the unicode61 tokenizer.
2) WebChat (Day 10): Discovered poor Chinese search performance, migrated to the trigram tokenizer, and added a LIKE fallback.
3) Slack (Day 12): Built a Boolean search engine (AND/OR/NOT) on top of this foundation.
qa_annotations.ground_truth:
1) Slack (Day 2): Initially created an FTS5 virtual table using the unicode61 tokenizer.
2) WebChat (Day 10): Discovered poor Chinese search performance, migrated to the trigram tokenizer, and added a LIKE fallback.
3) Slack (Day 12): Built a Boolean search engine (AND/OR/NOT) on top of this foundation.

qa_annotations.question: What are the main third-party libraries and technologies used in the project?
qa_annotations.answer: Electron (application framework/Telegram), React+TypeScript (frontend/Discord), better-sqlite3 (database/Slack), CodeMirror 6 (editor/Discord), D3.js (graph visualization/Telegram), marked (Markdown rendering/Slack export), electron-store (settings/Telegram), uuid (ID generation/Slack).
qa_annotations.ground_truth: Electron (application framework/Telegram), React+TypeScript (frontend/Discord), better-sqlite3 (database/Slack), CodeMirror 6 (editor/Discord), D3.js (graph visualization/Telegram), marked (Markdown rendering/Slack export), electron-store (settings/Telegram), uuid (ID generation/Slack).
